title: Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, Discovery, and Traffic Policies · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Dns Service Discovery
summary: A frontend loses backends as Pods rotate because it pins Pod IPs; a Service selector overlaps a canary and traffic policies change source and locality behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s dns service discovery increment in service discovery and endpoint evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve namespace-qualified Service and Pod records, search paths, headless answers, readiness publication, caching, and application retry behavior.
Choose ClusterIP, headless, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName, or selectorless Service from discovery, exposure, platform, and security constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Dns Service Discovery · Theory K8s Dns Service Discovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Resolve namespace-qualified Service and Pod records, search paths, headless answers, readiness publication, caching, and application retry behavior.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Dns Service Discovery contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict K8s Dns Service Discovery · Theory K8s Dns Service Discovery.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for resolve namespace-qualified service and pod records, search paths, headless answers, readiness publication, caching, and application retry behavior. For Predict K8s Dns Service Discovery · Theory K8s Dns Service Discovery, keep K8s Dns Service Discovery connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Resolve namespace-qualified Service and Pod records, search paths, headless answers, readiness publication, caching, and application retry behavior. A defensible solution applies K8s Dns Service Discovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to service discovery and endpoint evidence map.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-services-endpoints-dns-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-services-endpoints-dns-2
    artifact: service-discovery.yaml
    decision: worked k8s services endpoints dns 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      type: ClusterIP
      selector: {app: status-api}
      endpoint-evidence: ready-serving-terminating
      dns: status-api.intake.svc.cluster.local
    verify: kubectl get service,endpointslice -l kubernetes.io/service-name=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject Pod IP pinning, short-name cross-namespace assumptions, Service-as-health-probe claims, or locality policy as automatic availability.
    repair: Align selectors, inspect EndpointSlice conditions, use qualified DNS, test bind and route layers, and document platform-specific exposure.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A short Service name resolves identically from every namespace and external client. This fails the K8s Dns Service Discovery contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
